rationale: Clothing-rail: 'gold' is an established fashion fabric colour for vivid metallic-yellow garments. Users DO shop for 'gold dress' or 'gold top'. The vivid yellow hex matches wardrobe gold expectations.
Clothing-rail: 'bronze' is a widely accepted fashion colour — 'bronze dress', 'bronze shimmer fabric'. The warm copper-brown hex matches fabric bronze expectations.

Hardware-counter name: 'brushed' is a metal-finish descriptor (brushed metal, brushed nickel). While 'pewter' alone is accepted fabric vocabulary, 'brushed pewter' reads as a hardware surface treatment.
Hardware-counter name: 'chrome' is unambiguously a metal/hardware finish term. 'Chrome silver' reads as polished metal, not fabric. Swatch is a very light cool grey best described as frost.
Clothing-rail: 'copper' is an established fashion colour — 'copper silk', 'copper sequin dress'. The warm brown-orange hex aligns with fabric copper expectations. Widely used in fashion vocabulary.
Clothing-rail: 'gunmetal' is an established fashion grey — 'gunmetal grey suit', 'gunmetal accessories'. Widely recognised as a dark neutral grey tone in fashion vocabulary.
Clothing-rail: 'olive' firmly anchors this as a fabric/earth tone. 'Olive gold' reads as a warm olive variant, not hardware. The green-gold hue matches wardrobe expectations.
reachability: trueAutumn accentColours; chart signature nearest for aquarius×trueSpring, aquarius×trueAutumn

rationale: Clothing-rail: 'pewter' is a well-established fabric grey tone — 'pewter silk', 'pewter bridesmaid dress'. Widely used in fashion for muted cool greys.
Clothing-rail: 'silver' is a staple fashion colour — 'silver gown', 'silver fabric'. Universally recognised as both a metal and a fabric colour. The hex matches standard silver expectations.
reachability: lightSummer + deepWinter + trueWinter accentColours; chart signature nearest for cancer×trueSummer

rationale: Hardware-counter name: while standalone 'copper' is kept as an accepted fashion term, 'soft copper' reads as a specific metal patina description. Swatch is a warm tan-brown better described as sienna.
Hardware-counter name: 'soft gold' beside a Metal Tone slider reads as a jewellery finish description. Swatch is a warm golden-yellow better described as a rich honey tone.
Clothing-rail: 'steel grey' is ubiquitous in fashion — 'steel grey blazer', 'steel grey wool'. The compound name is more fabric than metal despite the 'steel' keyword.
Hardware-counter name: 'antique brass' is a metal finish (door handles, fixtures). Swatch is a warm orange-brown near copper/sienna. 'Rich Sienna' describes the fabric tone accurately.

Hardware-counter name: 'burnished' is a metal-finish descriptor and 'gold' is a metal. Together they evoke polished hardware. Swatch is a warm amber tone. 'Tawny Amber' uses established fabric vocabulary.

Hardware-counter name: 'burnt copper' describes an oxidised metal surface. Swatch is a warm rust-red better described as an ember tone. Distinct from existing 'Ember' (sagittarius/warm) which is more orange.
Hardware-counter name: 'dark bronze' describes a darkened metal. Swatch is a deep brown closely matching umber/bark brown tones. 'Deep Umber' is a standard fabric colour description.

This accent's LCH produces a dark teal (#25515B), not the grey that 'pewter' implies. The library token 'pewter' (#8B8E90) is correctly grey and KEPT. This accent is misnamed — renaming to match its actual dark teal hue.
Hardware-counter name: 'platinum' is unambiguously a precious metal. Swatch is a cool blue. 'Frost Blue' describes the icy blue-grey fabric tone while keeping the 'frost' temperature cue.

Hardware-counter name: 'gold' in the compound creates metal association. 'Saffron' alone is a spice/fabric term. Replacing 'gold' with 'amber' preserves the warm tone while removing hardware vocabulary.
